title: Actor Alexander Morton from TV Show Dies at 81
summary: Alexander Morton, an actor from Scotland, has died at 81 years old. He was famous for playing Golly Mackenzie in the TV series Monarch of the Glen, which is a show about a big estate in the Highlands. He acted in all 64 episodes from 2000 to 2005. Morton also played other roles in movies and TV shows like Get Carter and Take The High Road. He started acting in the 1970s and worked for many years on stage and screen. People from BBC Scotland and his friend Robert Carlyle said they are sad and remember him as a kind and talented actor who helped make Scottish TV special. He will be missed by fans and friends.

background_read: Alexander Morton was a Scottish actor who became well-known for his role in Monarch of the Glen, a TV show that many people in Scotland and beyond enjoyed. He started acting in the 1970s and worked in London and Scotland, showing his talent in different plays and TV series. His friend Robert Carlyle praised him for being kind and a great actor, which helps us understand why people are sad about his passing.
